question: [File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._sendGasCompensation / systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where USDSGasCompensation was raised by timelock after N troves opened (each depositing oldGasComp to GasPool) trigger liquidateTroves(N), causing _sendGasCompensation to call usdsToken.returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, liquidator, N * newGasComp) where newGasComp > oldGasComp, violating INVARIANT that GasPool USDS paid out per liquidated trove must equal the amount deposited at trove open, causing scoped impact: liquidator receives excess USDS reward (newGasComp - oldGasComp) * N beyond entitlement? Local proof idea: add unit test with two USDSGasCompensation values (10e18 then 20e18), open 3 troves at 10e18, change param via mock timelock, liquidate all 3, assert liquidator received 60e18 instead of 30e18 and GasPool balance went negative (or reverted).

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: BorrowerOperations.closeTrove / TroveManager._sendGasCompensation] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS where USDSGasCompensation was decreased by timelock after trove opened (depositing oldGasComp=10e18 to GasPool) trigger closeTrove which burns only newGasComp=5e18 from GasPool, leaving 5e18 orphaned in GasPool, then a subsequent LIQUIDATOR liquidating a different trove receives newGasComp=5e18 from GasPool but the orphaned 5e18 remains unaccounted, violating INVARIANT that GasPool.balance == activeTroveCount * currentUSDSGasCompensation, causing scoped impact: GasPool accumulates excess USDS that can be claimed by future liquidators beyond their per-trove entitlement? Local proof idea: add invariant fuzz test asserting usdsToken.balanceOf(gasPool) == troveManager.getTroveOwnersCount() * systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation() after every open/close/liquidate sequence with varying gasComp values.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._redeemCloseTrove / usdsToken.burn] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS where USDSGasCompensation was raised from 10e18 to 20e18 after a trove opened (depositing 10e18 to GasPool) trigger redeemCollateral fully closing that trove, causing _redeemCloseTrove to call usdsToken.burn(gasPoolAddress, 20e18) when GasPool only holds 10e18, violating INVARIANT that GasPool must always have sufficient USDS to cover the current gas compensation burn for any fully-redeemed trove, causing scoped impact: revert that blocks redemption path, or if GasPool was inflated by donations, the burn consumes more than the trove's deposited share? Local proof idea: add unit test opening trove at gasComp=10e18, change to 20e18, attempt full redemption, assert revert or assert GasPool balance goes below zero accounting.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.batchLiquidateTroves / _addLiquidationValuesToTotals] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where batchLiquidateTroves is called with an array containing the same trove address duplicated trigger the loop to skip the duplicate (status != active after first liquidation) but still accumulate totalUSDSGasCompensation only once, then verify whether the single-entry accounting is correct, violating INVARIANT that totalUSDSGasCompensation sent from GasPool must equal exactly the number of successfully liquidated troves times USDSGasCompensation, causing scoped impact: if duplicate processing is inconsistent, liquidator could receive incorrect gas compensation? Local proof idea: add unit test calling batchLiquidateTroves([troveA, troveA, troveB]), assert liquidator receives exactly 2 * USDSGasCompensation and GasPool decreases by exactly 2 * USDSGasCompensation.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: USDSToken.returnFromPool / _requireCallerIsTroveMorSP] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where they control a contract registered as StabilityPool (impossible in production but testable) trigger usdsToken.returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, attacker, amount) to drain GasPool USDS to themselves, violating INVARIANT that only TroveManager liquidation paths may transfer USDS out of GasPool via returnFromPool, causing scoped impact: unauthorized extraction of USDS gas compensation reserve? Local proof idea: add unit test deploying a mock StabilityPool that calls returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, attacker, balance), assert the call succeeds and GasPool is drained, confirming the caller restriction is the only guard.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: USDSToken._requireValidRecipient / USDSToken.transfer] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where gasPoolAddress is NOT in the blocked recipient list of _requireValidRecipient trigger usdsToken.transfer(gasPoolAddress, amount) to inflate GasPool USDS balance beyond N * USDSGasCompensation, then when a liquidation occurs the liquidator receives exactly currentUSDSGasCompensation per trove (not the inflated amount), violating INVARIANT that GasPool balance should equal activeTroveCount * USDSGasCompensation, causing scoped impact: donated USDS is permanently locked in GasPool or creates accounting desync that future parameter changes could make extractable? Local proof idea: add unit test calling usdsToken.transfer(gasPool, 1e18) from a USDS holder, assert it succeeds, assert GasPool balance > activeTroveCount * gasComp, assert the excess is not claimable by any liquidator.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._liquidateRecoveryMode / TroveHelper.getCappedOffsetVals] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where a trove has ICR >= MCR and ICR < TCR and entireTroveDebt <= USDSInStabPool trigger _liquidateRecoveryMode which calls getCappedOffsetVals, which reads systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation() at liquidation time rather than at trove-open time, violating INVARIANT that USDSGasCompensation paid from GasPool must match the amount deposited when the trove opened, causing scoped impact: if gasComp was raised, liquidator receives excess USDS from GasPool beyond the trove's deposited share? Local proof idea: add unit test opening trove at gasComp=10e18, manipulate system into recovery mode, raise gasComp to 15e18, liquidate via recovery mode capped path, assert liquidator received 15e18 from GasPool but only 10e18 was deposited.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.liquidate / _sendGasCompensation / ActivePool.sendBNB] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR-CONTRACT under PRECONDITIONS where the liquidator is a contract with a malicious receive() function trigger _sendGasCompensation which first calls usdsToken.returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, liquidator, USDS) then calls activePool.sendBNB(liquidator, BNB), causing the BNB send to invoke liquidator.receive() which re-enters liquidateTroves or batchLiquidateTroves, violating INVARIANT that gas compensation from GasPool must be paid exactly once per liquidated trove, causing scoped impact: double-claim of USDS gas compensation from GasPool in a single transaction? Local proof idea: add reentrancy unit test where liquidator.receive() calls liquidateTroves(1) again, assert GasPool USDS decreases by exactly 1 * USDSGasCompensation total (not 2x).

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: BorrowerOperations.openTrove / systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS where USDSGasCompensation is read twice in openTrove (once for compositeDebt calculation and once for the GasPool mint) with a potential TOCTOU if systemState is updated between calls trigger a mismatch where compositeDebt includes oldGasComp but GasPool receives newGasComp, violating INVARIANT that the gas compensation minted to GasPool must exactly equal the gas compensation component of the trove's recorded debt, causing scoped impact: GasPool balance diverges from trove debt accounting, enabling future liquidators to claim more or less than the trove's debt-backed gas compensation? Local proof idea: add differential test checking that for every openTrove, the increase in GasPool balance equals the increase in trove debt minus the net USDS minted to borrower.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.liquidateTroves / _getTotalsFromLiquidateTrovesSequence_NormalMode] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where liquidateTroves(N) is called with N larger than the number of eligible troves trigger the loop to process fewer troves than N but still call _sendGasCompensation with totalUSDSGasCompensation equal to the actual count processed, violating INVARIANT that the USDS sent from GasPool must equal exactly the number of troves actually liquidated times USDSGasCompensation, causing scoped impact: if loop boundary or counting is off-by-one, liquidator receives incorrect gas compensation from GasPool? Local proof idea: add fuzz test with random N values from 0 to 100 against a pool of K < N eligible troves, assert GasPool decrease == min(N,K) * USDSGasCompensation.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._liquidateRecoveryMode / TroveOwners.length guard] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where TroveOwners.length == 1 (single active trove, current live state) trigger liquidateTroves(1) in recovery mode, causing _liquidateRecoveryMode to return zero values (due to TroveOwners.length <= 1 guard), then _addLiquidationValuesToTotals adds zero USDSGasCompensation, and _sendGasCompensation sends 0 USDS from GasPool, violating INVARIANT that the last-trove guard must prevent any gas compensation from being paid when no liquidation actually occurs, causing scoped impact: confirm no USDS is incorrectly extracted from GasPool in the single-trove recovery mode boundary? Local proof idea: add unit test with exactly 1 trove in recovery mode, call liquidateTroves(1), assert GasPool balance unchanged and liquidator received 0 USDS.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._liquidateNormalMode vs _liquidateRecoveryMode] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where TroveOwners.length == 1 trigger liquidate(singleTrove) in NORMAL mode (which has no TroveOwners.length <= 1 guard in _liquidateNormalMode), causing the single trove to be liquidated and _sendGasCompensation to pay USDSGasCompensation from GasPool, violating INVARIANT that the last-trove protection should be consistent across all liquidation paths, causing scoped impact: liquidator extracts gas compensation from GasPool by liquidating the last trove in normal mode when recovery mode would have blocked it? Local proof idea: add unit test with 1 trove below MCR in normal mode, call liquidate(), assert GasPool decreases by USDSGasCompensation and liquidator receives it.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._redeemCloseTrove / usdsToken.burn(gasPoolAddress)] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS where multiple troves are fully redeemed in a single redeemCollateral call trigger _redeemCloseTrove for each fully-redeemed trove, each burning v.usdsGasCompensation from GasPool, violating INVARIANT that the total USDS burned from GasPool across all redemption-closed troves must equal the number of closed troves times USDSGasCompensation and GasPool must not go below zero, causing scoped impact: if GasPool has insufficient balance due to prior parameter changes, redemption reverts or burns more than deposited? Local proof idea: add unit test redeeming across 3 troves fully, assert GasPool decreases by exactly 3 * USDSGasCompensation and does not underflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: BorrowerOperations.closeTrove / _repayUSDS(gasPoolAddress)] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS where USDSGasCompensation was increased from 10e18 to 20e18 after trove opened trigger closeTrove which reads current USDSGasCompensation=20e18 and attempts to burn 20e18 from GasPool (which only holds 10e18 for this trove), violating INVARIANT that closeTrove must always be able to burn exactly the gas compensation from GasPool without reverting, causing scoped impact: closeTrove reverts if GasPool balance is insufficient due to parameter increase, trapping borrower's collateral? Local proof idea: add unit test opening trove at gasComp=10e18, raise to 20e18, call closeTrove, assert revert with insufficient GasPool balance.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._sendGasCompensation / usdsToken.returnFromPool] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where GasPool USDS balance was inflated by a direct usdsToken.transfer(gasPoolAddress, extraAmount) from any USDS holder trigger liquidateTroves(N) which sends exactly N * currentUSDSGasCompensation from GasPool (not the inflated amount), violating INVARIANT that the inflated GasPool balance creates a permanent surplus that no protocol path can recover, causing scoped impact: confirm whether the surplus USDS is permanently locked or whether any future liquidation/parameter-change sequence allows a liquidator to extract it? Local proof idea: add unit test donating 50e18 to GasPool, liquidate 1 trove, assert liquidator received exactly 10e18 (not 60e18), assert 50e18 remains locked in GasPool.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.liquidateTroves / _getTotalsFromLiquidateTrovesSequence_RecoveryMode backToNormalMode transition] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where the system starts in recovery mode and transitions to normal mode mid-sequence trigger _getTotalsFromLiquidateTrovesSequence_RecoveryMode where backToNormalMode flips to true, causing subsequent troves to be liquidated via normal-mode logic within the same loop, violating INVARIANT that USDSGasCompensation accounting must be consistent regardless of mode transition mid-sequence, causing scoped impact: if mode transition causes double-counting or skipping of USDSGasCompensation for some troves, liquidator receives incorrect gas compensation from GasPool? Local proof idea: add unit test with mixed ICR troves spanning recovery/normal mode boundary, assert totalUSDSGasCompensation == count_of_actually_liquidated_troves * USDSGasCompensation.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.batchLiquidateTroves / _getTotalsFromBatchLiquidate_RecoveryMode] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where batchLiquidateTroves is called with an array containing a mix of active and already-closed troves trigger the loop to skip non-active troves (status != active check) but still accumulate totalUSDSGasCompensation only for active ones, violating INVARIANT that GasPool USDS paid must equal exactly the count of successfully liquidated troves times USDSGasCompensation, causing scoped impact: if the skip logic is incorrect and a closed trove's USDSGasCompensation is counted, liquidator extracts excess USDS from GasPool? Local proof idea: add unit test with array [activeTrove, closedTrove, activeTrove2], assert GasPool decreases by exactly 2 * USDSGasCompensation.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._liquidateNormalMode / _getOffsetAndRedistributionVals] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where StabilityPool has exactly 0 USDS deposits trigger _liquidateNormalMode where _getOffsetAndRedistributionVals returns debtToOffset=0 and all debt is redistributed, but USDSGasCompensation is still set to systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation() and paid from GasPool, violating INVARIANT that gas compensation from GasPool must be paid regardless of whether debt is offset or redistributed, causing scoped impact: confirm liquidator always receives full USDSGasCompensation from GasPool even when StabilityPool is empty and all debt is redistributed? Local proof idea: add unit test with empty StabilityPool, liquidate trove, assert liquidator received USDSGasCompensation from GasPool.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._liquidateRecoveryMode ICR <= 100% branch] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where a trove has ICR <= 100% in recovery mode trigger _liquidateRecoveryMode which sets debtToOffset=0 and collToSendToSP=0 (pure redistribution) but still sets USDSGasCompensation=systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation() and pays it from GasPool, violating INVARIANT that gas compensation from GasPool must be paid even in pure-redistribution recovery mode liquidations, causing scoped impact: confirm GasPool correctly pays USDSGasCompensation to liquidator for ICR<=100% recovery mode troves? Local proof idea: add unit test with ICR=50% trove in recovery mode, liquidate, assert liquidator received USDSGasCompensation from GasPool and GasPool balance decreased correctly.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._redeemCollateralFromTrove / _redeemCloseTrove] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS where a trove's debt is exactly USDSGasCompensation + minNetDebt trigger _redeemCollateralFromTrove with maxUSDSamount = minNetDebt, causing newDebt == USDSGasCompensation and triggering _redeemCloseTrove which burns USDSGasCompensation from GasPool, violating INVARIANT that the full redemption path must burn exactly USDSGasCompensation from GasPool (not more, not less), causing scoped impact: if the equality check v.newDebt == v.usdsGasCompensation uses a stale gasComp value, the wrong amount is burned from GasPool? Local proof idea: add unit test with trove debt = gasComp + minNetDebt, redeem minNetDebt, assert GasPool burned exactly current gasComp and trove is closed.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._redeemCollateralFromTrove / _redeemCloseTrove vs BorrowerOperations.closeTrove] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS where USDSGasCompensation changed between trove open and redemption trigger _redeemCloseTrove which burns currentGasComp from GasPool (not the deposited amount), while a voluntary closeTrove also burns currentGasComp, violating INVARIANT that both close paths must burn the same amount from GasPool and that amount must equal what was deposited, causing scoped impact: parameter change creates asymmetry where one close path drains GasPool faster than the other relative to deposits? Local proof idea: add differential test comparing GasPool balance change for closeTrove vs full redemption close with same trove state and same gasComp change, assert both paths produce identical GasPool balance changes.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._sendGasCompensation / usdsToken.returnFromPool caller=TroveManager] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where they observe that returnFromPool is callable by both TroveManager and StabilityPool trigger a scenario where StabilityPool's _sendUSDSToDepositor calls returnFromPool(address(this), depositor, amount) from StabilityPool's own balance (not GasPool), confirm that StabilityPool cannot be tricked into calling returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, attacker, amount), violating INVARIANT that only TroveManager liquidation paths may drain GasPool via returnFromPool, causing scoped impact: unauthorized USDS extraction from GasPool via StabilityPool's returnFromPool call? Local proof idea: add unit test verifying StabilityPool._sendUSDSToDepositor always uses address(this) as poolAddress, never gasPoolAddress.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.liquidateTroves / _getTotalsFromLiquidateTrovesSequence_NormalMode] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where liquidateTroves(0) is called trigger the loop with _n=0 iterations, causing totals.totalDebtInSequence=0 and hitting the assert(totals.totalDebtInSequence > 0) revert, violating INVARIANT that no gas compensation should be paid from GasPool when no troves are liquidated, causing scoped impact: confirm the zero-trove boundary correctly prevents any GasPool drain? Local proof idea: add unit test calling liquidateTroves(0), assert revert and GasPool balance unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.liquidate / _liquidateNormalMode vs _liquidateRecoveryMode] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS where the system is at the exact TCR == CCR boundary trigger liquidate() where _checkRecoveryMode returns different results depending on gas/ordering, causing the wrong liquidation path to be taken, violating INVARIANT that the mode determination at the start of liquidate() must be consistent with the mode used for gas compensation calculation, causing scoped impact: if normal mode is used when recovery mode should apply (or vice versa), the USDSGasCompensation paid from GasPool may differ from what the trove's debt accounting expects?
